danger_signals.visual_form: Visual wind-up animations specific to each boxer (e.g., Bald Bull's distinctive Bull Charge stance)
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent high-damage punch incoming; player must counter or dodge

danger_signals.signal: Little Mac color shift to pink/exhausted appearance
danger_signals.visual_form: Mac's sprite palette changes to lighter pink/washed-out colors
danger_signals.meaning: Heart counter depleted; Mac is exhausted and cannot attack, vulnerability state

danger_signals.visual_form: Mac falls to canvas and struggles to rise within 10-second count
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent loss if knocked down 3 times per round or 4 times total in bout

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent raises hands/gloves to face in defensive posture
danger_signals.meaning: Face punches will be blocked; body blows required instead (gutter opportunity)

danger_signals.visual_form: Golden star appears above/near Mac after successful counter-punch
danger_signals.meaning: Powerful uppercut now available; limited resource (max 3 stars)

safety_signals.visual_form: Opponent drops hands, neutral stance visible
safety_signals.meaning: Window to land face punches safely without block

safety_signals.visual_form: Mac moves left/right or crouches while opponent punch whiffs past
safety_signals.meaning: Attack avoided; heart counter not depleted; counter-punch opportunity
Heart counter is healthy; Mac can attack and defend normally

safety_signals.signal: Pre-fight entrance music and opponent introduction
safety_signals.visual_form: Character enters ring with country-specific theme music; Mario referee present
safety_signals.meaning: Match beginning; no danger yet, establishment phase

safety_signals.visual_form: Mac stands upright after rising from canvas within 10-second count
safety_signals.meaning: Match continues; opportunity to regain hearts by dodging opponent attacks

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied (Glass Joe: yellow/blonde, Don Flamenco: pink/red tones, Piston Honda: yellow, Bald Bull: blue shorts)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rank within Minor Circuit progression; lower difficulty opponents
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: All use simplified animation sets; fewer attack patterns than World Circuit versions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Minor Circuit ring has distinct HUD palette; lower-tier arena aesthetics

faction_indicators.color_identity: Enhanced/darker color schemes (e.g., Von Kaiser, King Hippo, Bald Bull Major variant)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mid-tier ranking badge; opponents show increased complexity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Same character sprites as Minor but with palette changes; expanded attack repertoires
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Major Circuit ring palette differs from Minor; slightly more ornate presentation

faction_indicators.color_identity: International/exotic color palettes (Great Tiger: orange/purple, Don Flamenco World: enhanced colors, Super Macho Man: bright color scheme)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Reappearances with new attack patterns; completely redesigned move sets forcing new strategies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: World Circuit ring has most ornate HUD and arena design; championship prestige visual language

faction_indicators.color_identity: Black with white/gold accents (Tyson's real-world appearance translated to sprite)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Title bout designation; final boss status; undisputed world heavyweight champion aura
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unique sprite set not shared with other fighters; distinctive stance and attack patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dream Fight bout has special presentation; separate password access (007 373 5963 for Tyson); no ring-entrance music (builds tension)
Mario presence in all bouts signifies official match legitimacy; maintains visual continuity across circuits

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player ascending circuit ranks (Minor → Major → World)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive HUD palette changes; opponent sprite complexity increases; ring aesthetics enhance; championship tier implied through visual hierarchy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents gain new attack patterns; previously-defeated opponents reappear with redesigned strategies; difficulty progression tied to visual sophistication

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent throws punch with telegraphed animation; Mac's counter-punch lands visually before opponent guard raises
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Star earned; opponent temporarily stunned; clock stops (in TAS exploits); reset to neutral stance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mac sprite palette shifts to pink/washed-out appearance; pose becomes slumped; visual fatigue indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mac cannot attack; can only dodge/duck/block; opponent presses advantage with unblocked attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent sprite falls to canvas; 10-second count timer displayed visually; Mac stands over downed opponent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Match pauses for knockout sequence; opponent attempts to rise; if fails to rise within 10 seconds, automatic KO loss

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Three knockdowns achieved in single round
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual TKO indicator (technical knockout condition met); match ends without full 10-second count
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Automatic win condition triggered; opponent cannot continue

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mac knocked down fourth time in match
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mac unable to rise sprite/animation plays; referee raises arm in loss gesture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Automatic knockout loss; match ends; ranking penalty applied if title bout loss

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Round counter visible; clock depletes through rounds; no knockout achieved
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Point accumulation determines winner; some opponents cannot be defeated by decision (auto-loss condition)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Limited Edition vs. Mike Tyson version
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character palette changes (Piston Honda and King Hippo: pinkish to yellow; Glass Joe: hair color change; Bald Bull: purple to light blue shorts); title screen differences; newspaper ending variant
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss is Super Macho Man (Limited Ed.) vs. Mike Tyson (public version); passworded 'Another World Circuit' mode; different ending narrative

environmental_storytelling.example: Glass Joe enters to 'La Marseillaise' (French anthem); Piston Honda to 'Sakura' (Japanese folk); Von Kaiser and Super Macho Man to Wagner's 'Ride of the Valkyries' (European prestige signal)

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character nationality encoded in sprite design and color palette
environmental_storytelling.example: Visual nationality communicated through hair color, clothing style, and color scheme rather than explicit text; player recognizes 'foreignness' through design language
Ring HUD palette progression from Minor to Major to World; background and arena aesthetics shift subtly to imply championship-level prestige and international scope

environmental_storytelling.technique: Opponent behavioral patterns as visual-kinetic storytelling
environmental_storytelling.example: Each boxer has signature attack animation sequence that teaches player through observation; repeated pattern recognition is core gameplay loop; visual tells replace dialogue

environmental_storytelling.technique: Mario referee presence as authority signifier
environmental_storytelling.example: Mario's consistent presence across all bouts (Minor, Major, World, Dream) maintains visual continuity and legitimacy; his sprite presence is world-state confirmation
Final screen displays newspaper headline and text communicating player's championship achievement; text Engrish corrections between Limited and public versions reflect localization story

environmental_storytelling.technique: Star uppercut as visual resource management
environmental_storytelling.example: Golden star sprite above Mac is concrete, visible resource; player sees max 3 stars simultaneously; star loss on hit or knockdown is visually immediate feedback

environmental_storytelling.technique: Heart counter as stamina/health visual language
environmental_storytelling.example: Heart icons represent Mac's remaining stamina; depletion causes palette shift to pink/exhaustion; visual feedback loop replaces numeric health bar

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Distinctive slow punch animation with long telegraph; player learns to identify and counter easily; represents beginner-tier difficulty
Visual charging stance and move pattern; counter-punch at exact moment creates instant knockdown; signature attack becomes visual tutorial for pattern recognition
Opens mouth wide in vulnerable state; player must punch into open mouth for damage; unique visual weak-point indicator
Backs up before charging forward; World Circuit variant has clock-stopping glitch opportunity; behavioral change signals difficulty escalation
Holds Rose between rounds; visual arrogance indicator; behavioral change between circuits (Minor: easier; World: aggressive)
Opponent vanishes briefly and reappears; visual disorientation technique; no ring-entrance music (ominous silence); unique behavioral anomaly
Smiling mocking animation on win; alternate unused pointing-and-laughing animation exists in ROM (developer intent for additional arrogance visual)

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Super Macho Man (Major and World Circuit, Dream Fight in Limited Ed.)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Distinctive visual wind-up; uses 'Dizzy Destroyer' combo technique (gut-then-face combo); behavioral complexity increases in World Circuit variant
Unique attack pattern set not derived from other fighters; no ring-entrance music (signifies final boss prestige); no blocked punches possible (perfect defense), representing unmatched skill tier
Palette shift to pink; can dodge/duck/block but cannot attack; behavioral constraint forces defensive gameplay until hearts regenerate
Golden star visible above head; can perform powerul uppercut once; star loss on hit/knockdown removes capability immediately
Hands/gloves shield face; face punches blocked; body blows penetrate guard (gutter mechanic); visual guard position directly communicates vulnerability window

notes: Mike Tyson's Punch-Out!! uses minimal HUD and relies on sprite-based visual communication for world-state. The game encodes difficulty, nationality, and match status through character sprite design, palette shifts, animation choreography, and arena aesthetics. No numeric health bars exist; instead, heart icons and pink color shifts communicate stamina. Faction progression (Minor → Major → World Circuit) is signaled through subtle ring palette changes and opponent complexity. The game's core mechanic—learning opponent attack patterns through visual observation—makes this primarily a visual communication game. Regional differences (Limited Edition vs. public Tyson version) include palette corrections and character color changes, reflecting localization effort. The absence of dialogue requires all narrative to flow through sprite design, animation, and environmental detail. Mario's referee presence serves as consistent world-state anchor across all bouts.
